compare_elastic: 2×2 mode-isolation study
verdict: AMPLITUDE HYPOTHESIS REJECTED — investigate per-mode source bug

grid: 32x32  Nt=200  dt=4.330127e-08 s
medium: cp=2000.0 cs=800.0 rho=1200.0
source: ux plane at x_1b=9  f=1.0 MHz

Pair-wise metrics (py_mode vs jl_mode):
  matched py_additive  vs  jl_additive: r=+0.4339  rms_ratio=0.8819  PSNR=9.92 dB  peak_ratio=1.6311  rmse=9.314e-07 m/s
  crossed py_additive  vs  jl_dirichlet: r=+0.3734  rms_ratio=2.7260  PSNR=1.92 dB  peak_ratio=5.0390  rmse=7.573e-07 m/s
  crossed py_dirichlet  vs  jl_additive: r=+0.5164  rms_ratio=0.1466  PSNR=10.58 dB  peak_ratio=0.2887  rmse=8.635e-07 m/s
  matched py_dirichlet  vs  jl_dirichlet: r=+0.5404  rms_ratio=0.4531  PSNR=11.43 dB  peak_ratio=0.8920  rmse=2.533e-07 m/s

Amplitude hypothesis acceptance:
  peak_ratio ∈ [0.7, 1.4]
  (Pearson r informational only; threshold 0.4 for context — phase drift expected from numerical-scheme differences.)

Detail:
Matched-mode peak-amplitude check FAILS for: additive/additive: peak_ratio=1.631. Peak ratios outside [0.7, 1.4] indicate a real source-scaling divergence between the two engines, not just a mode mismatch. Investigate whether one engine applies an additional normalization (dt, density, or 1/2-factor) to the source signal.
